name: mnema
summary: Mnema is the shared, self-hosted context and memory platform used by AI agents across the private hub, plugin kit, and public distribution repositories.
current_focus: Knowledge corpus intentionally reset on 2026-07-16. Agents must rebuild durable decisions, how-to records, documents, relations, and session continuity from current evidence only.
decisions: 2026-07: ai-hub, mnema-kit, and ai-hub-public share one canonical Mnema project context; repository paths are aliases, not separate knowledge projects.
2026-07: SQLite remains the knowledge authority; external vector databases are rebuildable search projections.
next_steps: Let agents repopulate only durable, evidence-backed decisions, how-to records, preferences, project documents, and typed relations.
Keep transient task details out of memory and close every material session with session_log plus project_update when focus changes.
Build at least 50 human-reviewed held-out context cases before declaring the company retrieval release gate passed.
architecture: SQLite is the knowledge source of truth. Server-owned context_get combines project/session authority with lifecycle-filtered FTS5 and VectorStore evidence under provenance, trust, freshness, and token policies. sqlite-vec is the embedded default and fallback; Qdrant is an optional revision-guarded projection. Typed temporal relations and generation-aware sync preserve cross-device identity.
conventions: Use canonical project name mnema for ai-hub, mnema-kit, and ai-hub-public.
Preserve original-language memory; English canonical summaries are additive and provenance-labelled.
Semantic similarity never decides what is current; context_get owns authority and freshness.
Only durable cross-session knowledge belongs in memory; longer material belongs in RAG.
Relations must use stable UIDs, explicit types, confidence, validity, and provenance.
